conceptClusters.title: Retrieved rule syntax basic matching and heading hierarchy
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves valid rule boundaries, exact universal/type/class/ID match sets, and content-derived heading hierarchy before composing selectors.
Learner preserves meaningful headings and semantic source while CSS matches relationships, attributes, states, and render-tree fragments.

conceptClusters.title: Selector lists combinators logical matching and attribute contracts
exitEvidence: Learner predicts complete match sets for selector lists, compounds, descendants, children, adjacent siblings, subsequent siblings, and bounded logical pseudo-classes over changed trees.
Learner chooses presence, exact, token, language-prefix, prefix, suffix, or substring attribute matching plus explicit case policy from an attribute's semantic contract.

conceptClusters.title: Interaction form location language and structural state selection
exitEvidence: Learner activates and inspects keyboard, pointer, focus, form, target, language, disclosure, and structural states rather than forcing a look with permanent classes.
Learner distinguishes mutually compatible, mutually exclusive, unavailable, privacy-limited, and structure-dependent states through changed behavior evidence.

conceptClusters.title: Pseudo-element fragments semantic lists markers and counters
exitEvidence: Learner uses pseudo-elements only for bounded presentation while required content, names, instructions, controls, and heading relationships remain in HTML.
Learner styles markers and counter representations while preserving ordered, unordered, nested, reversed, start, item-value, changed-insertion, copy, linearization, and named-environment evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked selector evidence board connects valid rules, semantic headings, exact tree and attribute matches, real state transitions, render-tree fragments, and semantic list sequences across changed source.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives one unfamiliar semantic service tree, selector candidates, state controls, attribute values, pseudo-element render traces, list mutations, and competing explanations without accepted answers.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner reads, predicts, inspects, and explains all eight new or retrieved concepts through ten committed match, state, fragment, and sequence decisions.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts the committed rule, tree, attribute, state, fragment, list, or hierarchy claim with one direct source, DOM, match, computed, render, or task observation.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one dependency at a time: valid rule, basic subject, heading meaning, list or relationship grammar, attribute contract, state source, fragment boundary, then sequence evidence.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss routes to the earliest incorrect match condition and requires a parallel changed tree, value, state, or list before continuing.

interactions.title: Read valid composed rule boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Read a composed style rule and label selector list, complex selector, compound selector, simple selector, combinator, declaration block, declarations, properties, and values.
interactions.artifactChange: Composed rule grammar map is saved.

evidence.observable: Every selector and rule unit is labeled by parsing and matching role without confusing commas, whitespace, compounds, or declarations.
evidence.changedCase: A second complex selector and another declaration are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every space separates selectors in a list and every comma means descendant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace commas, combinators, compound boundaries, and declaration braces separately.

interactions.title: Predict retained basic match sets
interactions.learnerAction: Predict exact universal, type, reusable-class, and unique-identifier matches before relationship and state conditions are added.

evidence.observable: Every basic selector has an exact subject set and duplicate identity is exposed before composition.
evidence.changedCase: A new semantic element repeats one class and copies one identifier.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Composed selectors can repair an incorrect basic subject without understanding its initial match set.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate literal type, class-token, and identifier conditions first.

interactions.title: Inspect hierarchy without generated headings
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect heading source, rendered text, heading navigation, and style-disabled outline before rejecting a pseudo-element proposal that invents missing section titles.
interactions.artifactChange: Heading and generated-content boundary is recorded.

evidence.observable: Content-derived heading ranks remain explicit HTML and presentation fragments do not create missing semantic sections.
evidence.changedCase: A subsection moves under a different parent and receives a new persistent title.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generated text styled like a heading is equivalent to a semantic heading.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair content hierarchy in HTML, then style its existing element or decorative fragment.

interactions.title: Predict selector-list union
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the union matched by two comma-separated complex selectors and contrast it with one descendant selector over the same changed tree.

evidence.observable: Comma-separated branches match either complete branch while whitespace requires an ancestor-descendant relationship.
evidence.changedCase: One matching type moves outside the named ancestor.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comma and whitespace are interchangeable grouping punctuation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate each selector-list branch independently, then union its subjects.

interactions.title: Inspect four combinator relations
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect descendant, child, next-sibling, and subsequent-sibling match traces and name the exact element relationship each requires.
interactions.artifactChange: Four relationship traces are attached to tree.

evidence.observable: Each matched subject has the required ancestry, parentage, adjacency, or prior-sibling relation and unrelated nodes remain excluded.
evidence.changedCase: A wrapper and an intervening sibling are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All combinators mean some related element appears anywhere nearby.
interactions.correctionRoute: Walk exact tree edges from final subject toward preceding selector.

interactions.title: Predict seven attribute operators
interactions.learnerAction: Predict complete presence, exact, whitespace-token, language-prefix, prefix, suffix, and substring match sets across empty, case-changed, multi-token, path, query, and fragment values.
interactions.artifactChange: Attribute operator truth table is committed.

evidence.observable: Every accepted and rejected value follows the chosen operator's exact string or token contract and declared case policy.
evidence.changedCase: Values gain uppercase text, empty strings, extra tokens, queries, and fragments.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Substring matching safely represents every attribute contract.
interactions.correctionRoute: State semantic value grammar before selecting the narrowest operator.

interactions.title: Inspect actual pseudo-class transitions
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect focus, focus-visible, hover, active, target, open, checked, invalid, language, and child-index state changes without adding state classes.
interactions.artifactChange: State source and transition map is saved.

evidence.observable: Each pseudo-class match begins and ends only when its browser, document, form, URL, language, or structure condition changes.
evidence.changedCase: Input method, URL fragment, disclosure state, validity, and sibling position all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Writing a pseudo-class causes the named behavior or state to exist.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify state owner and trigger before inspecting selector match.

interactions.title: Explain pseudo-element fragment boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Explain originating element, render-tree fragment, generated box, supported property, unavailable-content case, and why required names or instructions stay in HTML.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes DOM content from ::before, ::after, ::marker, ::first-line, ::first-letter, ::selection, and ::placeholder fragments.
evidence.changedCase: Generated presentation is suppressed and user selects or copies content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-elements are semantic child nodes equivalent to authored elements.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare DOM, render tree, accessibility evidence, copied text, and style-disabled task.

interactions.title: Inspect semantic list and marker sequence
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect ordered, unordered, nested, reversed, start-value, item-value, marker-box, counter, copied-text, and style-disabled list evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: List meaning and marker sequence trace is saved.

evidence.observable: HTML determines list meaning and item order while CSS changes marker and counter representation without inventing membership.
evidence.changedCase: An item is inserted, nesting changes, and ordered list becomes reversed with a new start.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible numbering alone proves semantic ordered-list relationships.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect HTML list ownership and tree order before marker rendering.

interactions.title: Explain complete selector evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Explain rule validity, subject, relationship, attribute contract, real state, originating fragment, semantic list, marker sequence, and heading invariant in one changed artifact.
interactions.artifactChange: Integrated selector model becomes checkpoint evidence.

evidence.observable: Every conclusion names its exact source or browser condition, complete subject set, changed mutation, and evidence limit.
evidence.changedCase: Tree nesting, attribute values, focus, URL, generated content, list order, and heading scope change together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A matching screenshot proves every selector and semantic dependency.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct conditions from final subject outward and verify each layer separately.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An equipment-loan service gains original relationship, attribute, state, pseudo-element, marker, and counter rules through small predictions, edits, inspection, tests, explanations, saved checkpoints, and changed inventory records.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives complete semantic equipment-loan HTML, first-rule stylesheet evidence, real inventory states, nested lists, interactive controls, empty advanced selector regions, and no completed selector solutions.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner authors, inspects, mutates, tests, and explains exact selector behavior while preserving heading hierarchy, HTML state meaning, keyboard evidence, list relationships, and saved source.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the observed rule, tree, attribute, state, fragment, sequence, heading, or persistence fact and gives the smallest next inspection or bounded edit.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Three progressive hints identify the responsible condition, then its source or evidence surface, then one bounded selector form without exposing completed source.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed interaction preserves work, opens a smaller parallel equipment record, then requires a changed retry before return to cumulative service.

interactions.title: Predict valid equipment rule boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Predict how adding a selector list and declaration changes rule anatomy without moving properties outside their declaration block.

evidence.observable: Prediction preserves complete selector prelude, braces, declarations, and terminators before matching begins.
evidence.changedCase: A second selector branch and second declaration are added.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding a comma after a declaration extends the selector list.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate rule prelude from declaration block before inserting source.

interactions.title: Inspect equipment basic subjects
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect current type, reusable-class, and unique-identifier match sets for equipment cards, state labels, headings, links, and controls.

evidence.observable: Every subject is selected from literal type, class membership, or unique identity before relationship conditions.
evidence.changedCase: A second card repeats a class and one copied identifier is rejected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Relationship selectors can infer missing class membership from visual similarity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify basic subject tokens before adding surrounding conditions.

interactions.title: Test equipment heading hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Disable CSS and verify equipment page, collection, category, card, and process headings follow content hierarchy before selecting them.

evidence.observable: Heading ranks and navigation remain coherent independently of selector or generated presentation.
evidence.changedCase: One equipment category moves under a new collection section.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A pseudo-element or large font can repair missing heading structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair persistent heading source and rank before styling.

interactions.title: Preserve semantic selector hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Add only proportional equipment classes and identifiers needed for later selectors without replacing headings, lists, links, labels, or controls.
interactions.artifactChange: Semantic equipment source gains bounded hooks.

evidence.observable: Hooks encode reusable presentation or unique identity while native and heading semantics remain intact.
evidence.changedCase: One card changes semantic container while retaining reusable presentation role.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic containers make relationship selectors more reliable than semantic elements.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep task-appropriate element and add only needed token.

interactions.title: Explain selector evidence baseline
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why valid rules, exact basic subjects, and meaningful headings must be proven before descendant, state, or fragment selectors are debugged.

evidence.observable: Explanation orders rule parsing, semantic source, basic subject, then composed match conditions.
evidence.changedCase: A malformed rule and wrong heading selector produce similar appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Advanced selectors compensate for invalid rules and broken semantics.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify dependencies from source validity through subject identity.

interactions.title: Predict equipment selector-list union
interactions.learnerAction: Predict all equipment headings and status labels matched by two complete comma-separated selector branches.

evidence.observable: Subject set equals union of independently matched branches and does not require cross-branch ancestry.
evidence.changedCase: A status label moves outside a card while retaining its class.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comma joins selectors into one ancestry chain.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate each comma-delimited branch from final subject outward.

interactions.title: Author grouped equipment subjects
interactions.learnerAction: Author one selector list for two equipment subjects sharing a presentation requirement and keep each branch independently valid.
interactions.artifactChange: First equipment selector-list rule is saved.

evidence.observable: Both intended sets match, unrelated elements do not, and complete list remains valid.
evidence.changedCase: One branch gains a class condition and another keeps a type subject.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One invalid selector branch is safely ignored in every selector list.
interactions.correctionRoute: Parse and test each branch plus the complete list before saving.

interactions.title: Test invalid list branch
interactions.learnerAction: Make one regular selector-list branch invalid, inspect loss of the complete rule, then repair and contrast with a provided forgiving :is() argument.

evidence.observable: Regular list invalidates as a unit while invalid forgiving argument drops within :is() and remaining valid arguments still match.
evidence.changedCase: Invalid token moves between regular list branch and :is() argument.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All selector lists discard only invalid branch.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify grammar production before predicting recovery.

interactions.title: Predict descendant versus child
interactions.learnerAction: Predict card content matched by descendant and child combinators before an extra wrapper is inserted around equipment metadata.
interactions.artifactChange: Ancestry and parentage predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Descendant survives deeper nesting while child requires immediate parent relation.
evidence.changedCase: A metadata wrapper changes subject depth by one level.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Child and descendant combinators differ only in notation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count exact parent edges from subject to ancestor.

interactions.title: Author exact child relation
interactions.learnerAction: Author an equipment card child selector when stakeholder requirement applies only to direct action groups and not nested action lists.

evidence.observable: Direct action groups match and nested action groups remain excluded after changed nesting.
evidence.changedCase: A nested maintenance action list is inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A broad descendant selector is harmless when current tree is shallow.
interactions.correctionRoute: Encode exact relationship promised by requirement and test added depth.

interactions.title: Predict adjacent and subsequent siblings
interactions.learnerAction: Predict equipment notes matched immediately after a status heading versus all later sibling notes under the same parent.

evidence.observable: Adjacent selector matches one immediately following sibling; subsequent selector matches qualifying later siblings sharing parent.
evidence.changedCase: An intervening paragraph and a nested note are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Sibling combinators match any elements appearing later in document.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify shared parent, order, and adjacency separately.

interactions.title: Test logical selector matching
interactions.learnerAction: Test bounded :is(), :where(), :not(), and :has() equipment selectors for exact subjects while deferring precedence calculations to the next module.
interactions.artifactChange: Logical selector match tests are checkpointed.

evidence.observable: Each logical selector's subject and accepted relative relation match the changed tree without unsupported specificity claims.
evidence.changedCase: A card gains, loses, and nests the relative child used by :has().

interactions.feedbackTarget: :has() selects descendants rather than its anchored subject, and :not() creates missing state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name functional pseudo-class subject, argument, anchor, and changed match set.

interactions.title: Predict attribute presence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict every equipment control with a disabled attribute present and distinguish presence from current visual appearance or a string value.

evidence.observable: Selector matches every element carrying attribute regardless of serialized value text while non-carriers remain excluded.
evidence.changedCase: Boolean attribute appears as empty, repeated-name, and omitted forms.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Presence selectors match only attributes whose value text is true.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect DOM attribute existence separately from state pseudo-class.

interactions.title: Author exact state-data match
interactions.learnerAction: Author an exact attribute selector for equipment availability data whose closed vocabulary requires one complete value.
interactions.artifactChange: Exact availability selector rule is saved.

evidence.observable: Only exact declared availability value matches; prefixes, suffixes, extra tokens, and empty values do not.
evidence.changedCase: Near-match values and uppercase variant enter fixture.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Substring selector is safer because it accepts future values automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use contract's closed vocabulary and reject accidental partial matches.

interactions.title: Test whitespace-token membership
interactions.learnerAction: Test equipment capability values containing multiple whitespace-separated tokens and prove token matching rejects embedded substrings.

evidence.observable: Whole whitespace token matches in any token order while longer embedded text and empty value do not.
evidence.changedCase: Token order changes and near-match token gains a prefix.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Token operator and substring operator accept same multi-value strings.
interactions.correctionRoute: Split attribute value on document-defined whitespace and compare whole tokens.

interactions.title: Test language-prefix contract
interactions.learnerAction: Test exact language value and hyphen-prefixed variants for equipment instructions without treating arbitrary text prefixes as language ranges.

evidence.observable: Exact language and hyphen-followed variants match while same-letter arbitrary extensions do not.
evidence.changedCase: Values change among en, en-US, enochian, and empty.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Language-prefix operator is a general starts-with operator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Apply exact-or-hyphen grammar rather than string prefix intuition.

interactions.title: Predict prefix suffix and substring
interactions.learnerAction: Predict equipment link matches for path prefix, file suffix, and query substring across absolute paths, queries, fragments, empty values, and deceptive filenames.

evidence.observable: Each operator tests literal attribute value position and excludes empty-query assumptions and deceptive near matches.
evidence.changedCase: URLs gain query, fragment, uppercase extension, and percent-encoded text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Suffix selector sees parsed file type and ignores queries or fragments.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare literal serialized or DOM attribute string, not inferred URL semantics.

interactions.title: Inspect attribute case policy
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect default document-language comparison and explicit ASCII-insensitive i or case-sensitive s flags over equipment values.

evidence.observable: Matches follow actual HTML attribute contract and explicit flag rather than a universal case rule.
evidence.changedCase: Value changes case and selector toggles between default, i, and s.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All HTML attribute values are automatically case-insensitive.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify document-language rule, then apply explicit comparison flag when needed.

interactions.title: Mutate equipment attributes through DOM
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate presence, exact state, token membership, language, and URL attributes through the DOM and inspect live matched sets and computed results.
interactions.artifactChange: Live attribute mutation trace becomes checkpoint evidence.

evidence.observable: Each DOM mutation changes only selectors whose operator contract becomes true or false.
evidence.changedCase: Five attributes mutate without reloading document.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute selectors are evaluated only from initial serialized source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect current DOM attribute value before and after each mutation.

interactions.title: Defend equipment attribute operators
interactions.learnerAction: Defend each equipment attribute operator and case rule from accepted and rejected values without claiming selector creates semantic state.
interactions.artifactChange: Attribute contract defense closes operator checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every choice follows semantic value grammar, complete near-miss set, live mutation, and bounded evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Stakeholder expands one closed value into a multi-token vocabulary.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad operator is future-proof even when it accepts unrelated values.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite accepted and rejected value grammar, then choose operator.

interactions.title: Predict focus state differences
interactions.learnerAction: Predict focus, focus-visible, and focus-within matches for keyboard, pointer, and nested-control paths through equipment filters.

evidence.observable: Focus follows active input target, focus-visible follows user-agent indication, and focus-within includes containing filter group.
evidence.changedCase: Input method changes and focus moves between nested controls.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :focus-visible always equals :focus and :focus-within moves focus.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate focused element, indicator heuristic, and ancestor containment.

interactions.title: Author keyboard-visible focus styling
interactions.learnerAction: Author equipment focus treatment that preserves a visible keyboard indicator and optionally styles its containing group without hover dependence.
interactions.artifactChange: Focus and focus-within rules are saved.

evidence.observable: Keyboard focus is clearly visible, group state follows nested focus, and pointer hover is not required.
evidence.changedCase: Focus traverses every filter and action control after new control insertion.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hover styling is an equivalent keyboard focus indicator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Traverse keyboard path and inspect actual focus pseudo-class matches.

interactions.title: Test hover active and focus paths
interactions.learnerAction: Test pointer hover, pointer activation, keyboard activation, and focus persistence on equipment links and buttons without making information hover-only.

evidence.observable: Each state appears during its true interaction and all required information remains available on keyboard and touch paths.
evidence.changedCase: Pointer is unavailable and one action gains nested content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :hover means user intent and works identically on every touch device.
interactions.correctionRoute: Observe each input path and keep task information persistent.

interactions.title: Inspect link and target states
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect any-link, link, privacy-limited visited, and target states for equipment documentation links and current URL fragments.
interactions.artifactChange: Location-state evidence and privacy limit are recorded.

evidence.observable: Hyperlink and fragment states follow actual URL relationship while visited inspection conclusions remain privacy-bounded.
evidence.changedCase: Current fragment and link destination change while history state is not exposed as data.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :visited can be queried to reveal a user's browsing history.
interactions.correctionRoute: Limit conclusion to allowed rendering and current hyperlink or fragment behavior.

interactions.title: Test native disclosure open state
interactions.learnerAction: Open and close equipment details with keyboard and pointer, then verify :open styling follows native state rather than creating disclosure behavior.

evidence.observable: Rule matches only while native element is open and activation, focus, name, and content behavior remain HTML-owned.
evidence.changedCase: Open attribute changes from absent to present and user toggles state.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :open turns any generic container into a disclosure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use native state-bearing element, then style observed state.

interactions.title: Predict equipment form states
interactions.learnerAction: Predict enabled, disabled, read-only, read-write, required, optional, valid, invalid, in-range, out-of-range, checked, and indeterminate matches.

evidence.observable: Every match follows actual control semantics, current value, constraint, and state rather than attribute text coincidence.
evidence.changedCase: Control type, constraints, value, checkedness, disabledness, and mutability change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute presence and state pseudo-class always select identical controls.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect control semantics and current state separately from serialized attributes.

interactions.title: Test validation interaction timing
interactions.learnerAction: Compare invalid and user-invalid equipment form states before entry, after user interaction, after correction, and after reset.

evidence.observable: State styling follows validity and user-interaction conditions without becoming the only error message.
evidence.changedCase: Required value moves through untouched, invalid, corrected, and reset states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :invalid and :user-invalid have identical timing and provide error text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Observe browser state timeline and preserve persistent associated error information.

interactions.title: Test structural state mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, remove, reorder, and wrap equipment records while testing empty, first, last, only, nth-child, and nth-of-type selectors.

evidence.observable: Matches update from actual sibling tree and type-filtered index after every mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Text, wrapper, different element type, and same element type are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: nth-child counts only elements of the subject's type.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate element children, then apply optional subject or type filter.

interactions.title: Explain equipment state ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Explain browser, URL, document, form, language, structure, and user-action sources for every equipment pseudo-class used and reject impossible combinations.

evidence.observable: Each rule cites real state trigger, termination, compatible states, impossible states, and changed behavior evidence.
evidence.changedCase: One state source changes without markup and another changes through DOM mutation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-class names are commands that create their described state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify external state model before matching selector.

interactions.title: Predict pseudo-element render fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Predict ::before, ::after, ::first-letter, ::first-line, ::selection, ::placeholder, and ::marker fragments without adding DOM nodes.

evidence.observable: Each fragment has an originating element, conditional render boundary, and bounded applicable content or property behavior.
evidence.changedCase: Text wraps, selection changes, placeholder disappears, and marker type changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-elements append semantic child elements to the DOM.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source, DOM, render fragment, and computed pseudo-element separately.

interactions.title: Author decorative availability cue
interactions.learnerAction: Add a bounded decorative pseudo-element to an equipment state label whose persistent text already communicates availability.

evidence.observable: Generated fragment supplements existing text, has no control behavior, and can disappear without information loss.
evidence.changedCase: Generated content is suppressed and state wording changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Required availability word can live only in ::before content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep status in HTML and make pseudo-element redundant presentation.

interactions.title: Test generated-content absence
interactions.learnerAction: Suppress equipment ::before and ::after content, inspect accessibility and copied-text evidence in named environments, and complete every task.

evidence.observable: Required names, instructions, headings, states, and actions remain explicit and usable without generated presentation.
evidence.changedCase: One decorative symbol becomes unavailable and copy selection spans its originating element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Current screen-reader exposure proves generated text is a portable semantic replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound conclusion to named environment and move required information into HTML.

interactions.title: Inspect text and selection fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect first-line reflow, first-letter eligibility, user selection, placeholder visibility, and supported property behavior on equipment content.

evidence.observable: Fragments respond to layout, text, focus, input, and user selection rather than fixed source substrings.
evidence.changedCase: Viewport width, leading punctuation, entered value, and selected range change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: ::first-line always selects the same source characters.
interactions.correctionRoute: Change layout and content, then inspect computed fragment.

interactions.title: Defend pseudo-element choices
interactions.learnerAction: Defend each equipment pseudo-element from originating element, presentation purpose, property support, changed render, suppressed-content task, and accessibility limit.

evidence.observable: Every fragment is redundant presentation or named visual adaptation with required semantics remaining authored.
evidence.changedCase: User styles, text wrapping, generated-content support, and copy behavior differ.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any text visually generated by CSS is suitable for labels and instructions.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify content necessity, persist required information, then justify fragment.

interactions.title: Predict equipment list meaning
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which equipment procedures require ordered lists, which categories are unordered, and how tree order changes meaning before marker styling.

evidence.observable: Ordered procedures change meaning when reordered; unordered categories do not; list item ownership is explicit.
evidence.changedCase: A checklist becomes a mandatory sequence and two steps swap.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Numbered appearance makes any group an ordered list.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether changing item order changes document meaning.

interactions.title: Style equipment markers
interactions.learnerAction: Style semantic equipment list markers with list-style and ::marker while preserving item ownership, readable sequence, and long-marker behavior.

evidence.observable: Markers originate from list items, supported properties compute, and semantic list sequence remains unchanged.
evidence.changedCase: Marker text length and writing direction change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Replacing li elements with decorated paragraphs is equivalent marker styling.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep semantic list and target its marker box.

interactions.title: Test ordered-list changes
interactions.learnerAction: Test equipment procedure insertion, removal, reversed order, start value, item value, nested sequence, and copied or style-disabled output.

evidence.observable: Visible and semantic sequence reflect HTML order attributes and mutations without hard-coded generated numbers.
evidence.changedCase: Procedure becomes reversed, begins at five, and one item overrides value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generated ::before numbers stay correct after every insertion automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use semantic ordered list state and inspect actual marker sequence.

interactions.title: Build nested equipment counters
interactions.learnerAction: Build bounded nested section counters for an equipment manual while keeping heading text, ranks, and list membership explicit in HTML.

evidence.observable: Counter scope, reset, increment, nested representation, and heading hierarchy remain correct after insertion.
evidence.changedCase: A new section and nested subsection are inserted at two depths.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Counters can replace actual heading text and hierarchy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist heading meaning, then layer optional numbering presentation.

interactions.title: Test marker representation limits
interactions.learnerAction: Test built-in or bounded custom marker representation across zero, negative, large, unsupported-range, image-failure, long-content, and named browser cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Marker representation limit record is checkpointed.

evidence.observable: Sequence stays understandable when chosen representation cannot render, marker image fails, or marker becomes long.
evidence.changedCase: Counter moves beyond declared symbol range and marker image returns an error.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One successful short list proves marker system for every value and failure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test representation domain, alternate rendering, and semantic sequence separately.

interactions.title: Defend equipment selector system
interactions.learnerAction: Defend equipment rules from grammar, exact subjects, heading hierarchy, relationships, attribute contracts, real states, fragment limits, list semantics, counters, and changed evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Guided equipment selector defense becomes completion evidence.

evidence.observable: Every selector names subject, conditions, changed match, state source, semantic invariant, evidence limit, and rejected broader alternative.
evidence.changedCase: Inventory tree, values, focus, validity, fragments, list order, and manual sections all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Compact selector source and attractive output prove maintainability.
interactions.correctionRoute: Attach one direct changed observation to every matching and semantic claim.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A separate transit-status service gains relationship, attribute, state, pseudo-element, marker, and counter rules from partial evidence prompts without reusing equipment source, requirement order, or visual design.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives semantic transit notices, routes, status attributes, live controls, nested disruption steps, an incomplete stylesheet, and faded evidence slots.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner completes fifteen increasingly independent source, match, mutation, accessibility, and sequence outcomes across all new and retrieved concepts.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback states failed transit invariant and one evidence category without identifying final selector or repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Faded hints provide a concept cue, then a source or evidence region, then bounded syntax only after learner records causal explanation.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss opens a smaller transit fragment with a different tree, state, or list mutation before transfer back to cumulative artifact.

interactions.title: Complete transit prerequisite audit
interactions.learnerAction: Complete missing rule-anatomy, basic-subject, and heading-hierarchy evidence before evaluating transit relationship selectors.

evidence.observable: Rules parse, basic subjects are exact, and route or disruption headings remain coherent without CSS.
evidence.changedCase: A route section moves and one selector branch becomes malformed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Advanced selector behavior can be judged before source and hierarchy validity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair rule and semantic dependencies first.

interactions.title: Complete transit relationship rules
interactions.learnerAction: Author missing list, child, and sibling selector branches for transit notices while excluding nested and intervening near matches.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit relationship rule set is saved.

evidence.observable: Each branch matches exact ancestry, parentage, or sibling relation after wrappers and notes are inserted.
evidence.changedCase: Nested advisory and intervening update enter tree.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One broad descendant selector covers every relationship safely.
interactions.correctionRoute: Translate stakeholder relation into exact tree edge.

interactions.title: Test transit logical selectors
interactions.learnerAction: Test transit :is(), :not(), and :has() match subjects after a route gains, loses, or nests a disruption child.

evidence.observable: Functional selector subjects and anchored relations remain exact without precedence claims.
evidence.changedCase: Disruption child changes existence and depth.

interactions.feedbackTarget: :has() permanently marks a parent after a past child existed.
interactions.correctionRoute: Re-evaluate current relative selector from anchored subject.

interactions.title: Complete transit attribute table
interactions.learnerAction: Complete accepted and rejected values for transit presence, exact status, token capability, language-prefix, route-prefix, file-suffix, and query-substring selectors.
interactions.artifactChange: Transit operator truth table is complete.

evidence.observable: Each operator accepts only values allowed by its grammar, including explicit empty and case cases.
evidence.changedCase: Values gain extra tokens, mixed case, query, fragment, and empty strings.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All operators are interchangeable if current fixture is small.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive operator from semantic attribute grammar.

interactions.title: Test transit DOM attribute changes
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate transit status, capability tokens, language, and destination attributes through DOM and verify changed matched sets.

evidence.observable: Each live mutation changes only operator conditions whose current value becomes true or false.
evidence.changedCase: Four attributes update without document reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute selectors use initial source text only.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect current DOM value and literal operator contract.

interactions.title: Author transit focus states
interactions.learnerAction: Author keyboard-visible focus and group-focus treatment for route filters without hiding indicators or using hover as substitute.

evidence.observable: Focused control and containing group expose visible state across keyboard path.
evidence.changedCase: New nested filter enters focus order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hover state proves keyboard focus appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Traverse keyboard focus and inspect focus-visible and focus-within.

interactions.title: Test transit URL states
interactions.learnerAction: Change transit URL fragment and link destinations, then test target and link states without extracting visited-history data.

evidence.observable: Current target follows fragment and hyperlink states follow actual link semantics within privacy limits.
evidence.changedCase: Fragment changes twice and one anchor loses href.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every anchor matches :any-link and visited state is readable as application data.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify hyperlink source condition, current fragment, and privacy boundary.

interactions.title: Test transit form states
interactions.learnerAction: Test disabled, checked, required, invalid, user-invalid, in-range, and reset transitions in transit notification settings.

evidence.observable: Styles follow current native control states while persistent labels and errors remain separate.
evidence.changedCase: Control moves from untouched through invalid, corrected, checked, disabled, and reset.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-class style creates validation behavior and error explanation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect control state owner and retain HTML feedback.

interactions.title: Explain transit structural matches
interactions.learnerAction: Explain first, last, only, empty, nth-child, and nth-of-type results after transit notices are inserted, wrapped, reordered, and retyped.

evidence.observable: Explanation enumerates current element siblings and type-filtered positions for each mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Different-type and same-type siblings trade positions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: nth-of-type counts classes and nth-child ignores different element types.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate element children, then apply selector-specific filter.

interactions.title: Add transit decorative fragment
interactions.learnerAction: Add a redundant decorative pseudo-element to a transit status whose persistent wording already communicates meaning.

evidence.observable: Generated presentation can disappear without losing status, heading, name, or action.
evidence.changedCase: Status wording changes and generated content is suppressed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-element can replace visible persistent status text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist status first, then add redundant fragment.

interactions.title: Test transit render fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Test transit first-line reflow, selection, placeholder, marker, and generated-content absence across changed text and viewport.

evidence.observable: Each fragment follows current render or input condition and required content remains authored.
evidence.changedCase: Text wraps, selection changes, input fills, and marker type changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-element maps to stable source substring.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect originating element and live render condition.

interactions.title: Explain transit fragment limits
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why route names, disruption instructions, filter labels, headings, and status meaning remain HTML despite available generated content.

evidence.observable: Explanation connects persistent task information to semantic source and limits pseudo-elements to presentation.
evidence.changedCase: Generated content support and assistive-technology exposure differ by named environment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any currently announced generated text is a universal semantic replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bound environment claim and keep required task information authored.

interactions.title: Style transit disruption lists
interactions.learnerAction: Style unordered route categories and ordered disruption steps with markers while keeping list meaning and item ownership intact.

evidence.observable: Correct list types remain semantic and marker changes do not alter membership or order meaning.
evidence.changedCase: A category becomes a required sequence and gains nested steps.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Marker appearance determines list semantics.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose HTML list from order meaning, then style marker.

interactions.title: Test transit numbering mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Test insertion, reversal, start, item value, nesting, long marker, style disabling, and copied text for transit disruption sequences.

evidence.observable: Semantic and presented order remains understandable through all numbering and representation changes.
evidence.changedCase: List reverses, starts at eight, inserts two steps, and marker expands.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hard-coded generated numbers remain correct after list mutation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use semantic list state and inspect current marker sequence.

interactions.title: Explain transit selector variation
interactions.learnerAction: Explain transit rule validity, subjects, hierarchy, relationships, attribute values, state triggers, fragments, list meaning, counters, mutations, and evidence limits.
interactions.artifactChange: Faded transit defense becomes completion evidence.

evidence.observable: Every claim cites changed source or browser evidence and rejects overbroad matching or semantic substitution.
evidence.changedCase: Route structure, values, states, fragments, and disruption numbering change together.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Passing initial transit fixture proves transfer from equipment workshop.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tie each concept to unfamiliar changed observation and retained invariant.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A ten-case clinic diagnoses invalid, overbroad, underbroad, impossible, privacy-limited, semantic-loss, fragment, marker, and counter defects from first divergent evidence rather than selector replacement.
activityDesigns.startingState: Ten unrelated client traces look similarly over-styled or unchanged but fail in rule parsing, subject identity, hierarchy, tree relation, attribute contract, state source, fragment boundary, or list sequence.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner predicts, inspects, debugs, tests, and explains exact causes and minimal repairs while preserving all retained semantics and task evidence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports first contradiction between hypothesis and observed match or semantic evidence, never final selector or source.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints narrow from condition category to evidence surface to one suspicious boundary without revealing repair.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Wrong diagnosis must be falsified on a parallel changed tree, value, state, or sequence before learner revises original case.

interactions.title: Predict invalid rule versus wrong subject
interactions.learnerAction: Predict evidence distinguishing an invalid selector list from a valid rule whose type, class, or identifier subject matches nothing.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates missing parsed rule from present rule with empty subject set.
evidence.changedCase: Invalid token is repaired while class token is removed from DOM.

interactions.feedbackTarget: No visible match always means selector syntax is invalid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed rule presence before subject membership.

interactions.title: Inspect generated heading failure
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect client heading source and navigation to locate a section title supplied only by ::before and repair persistent hierarchy.
interactions.artifactChange: Semantic heading cause and repair are saved.

evidence.observable: Required title becomes authored heading with correct rank; generated decoration remains optional.
evidence.changedCase: Section moves under another heading and generated content is suppressed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Making generated text larger repairs heading hierarchy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore persistent heading element and derive rank from content.

interactions.title: Debug overbroad descendant match
interactions.learnerAction: Replace a client descendant selector that captures nested controls with the exact parent or sibling relationship required.

evidence.observable: Only intended direct or sibling subjects match after nested controls and wrappers are added.
evidence.changedCase: Another nested action group and wrapper enter tree.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adding another class to every descendant is best relationship repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Encode actual tree relation before adding hooks.

interactions.title: Test sibling interruption
interactions.learnerAction: Insert an intervening client node and prove adjacent-sibling rule stops matching while subsequent-sibling rule retains only same-parent later subjects.

evidence.observable: Adjacency and subsequent-sibling conditions diverge exactly after insertion.
evidence.changedCase: Intervening node moves between and outside shared parent.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Adjacent means anywhere later within section.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect shared parent, element order, and immediate neighbor.

interactions.title: Debug substring state collision
interactions.learnerAction: Replace client substring selector that mistakes unavailable for available with exact or token operator derived from state vocabulary.

evidence.observable: Accepted state values match and embedded near matches, empty values, and case variants follow explicit contract.
evidence.changedCase: Vocabulary adds temporarily-available and unavailable values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Substring is safe when desired word appears in every good value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate complete accepted and rejected vocabulary.

interactions.title: Debug impossible state selector
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose a valid client selector whose pseudo-class combination can never coexist, then split rules around actual state transitions.
interactions.artifactChange: Impossible state conjunction becomes valid state rules.

evidence.observable: Repaired rules match observable compatible states and never force state with permanent classes.
evidence.changedCase: Control moves between enabled, disabled, valid, invalid, focused, and blurred.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A valid selector string guarantees some element can match.
interactions.correctionRoute: Model state ownership and mutual exclusivity before editing.

interactions.title: Test hover-only information defect
interactions.learnerAction: Remove client information revealed only on hover, persist it in HTML, and verify keyboard, touch, focus, and pointer task equivalence.

evidence.observable: Required information remains persistent or equivalently reachable while hover stays optional presentation.
evidence.changedCase: Pointer hover becomes unavailable and keyboard focus order changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hover state is guaranteed on touch and keyboard devices.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist task information and test each supported input path.

interactions.title: Debug generated instruction
interactions.learnerAction: Move a required client instruction out of ::after into associated HTML while retaining a nonessential decorative fragment.

evidence.observable: Instruction remains visible, programmatically related, copied, and usable when generated content is absent.
evidence.changedCase: Instruction text changes and user styles suppress generated content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Providing alternative text in CSS makes required form instruction universally robust.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist required task content in document and bound CSS to presentation.

interactions.title: Debug hard-coded list numbering
interactions.learnerAction: Replace client ::before numbers that drift after insertion with semantic ordered-list or scoped counter behavior and verify sequence.
interactions.artifactChange: List numbering cause and repair are saved.

evidence.observable: Current tree order, reversed or start state, insertion, nesting, copy, and style-disabled evidence remain coherent.
evidence.changedCase: Two items insert and list reverses from a new start.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Renumbering hard-coded strings once proves future sequence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive numbers from semantic list or scoped counter state.

interactions.title: Explain clinic first divergences
interactions.learnerAction: Explain first divergence for invalid rule, wrong subject, broken hierarchy, broad relation, wrong operator, impossible state, hover-only content, generated instruction, and numbering drift.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic causal defense becomes completion evidence.

evidence.observable: Each diagnosis cites earliest contradiction, exact repair, retained semantics, changed retest, and evidence limit.
evidence.changedCase: Two independent defects combine into one similar visual symptom.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Longer or more specific-looking selectors are inherently safer repairs.
interactions.correctionRoute: Order grammar, source, subject, relation, value, state, fragment, and sequence evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently built museum-collection stylesheet starts from semantic collection HTML and first-rule skills, then proves relationship, attribute, state, fragment, marker, counter, hierarchy, and changed-case behavior.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives a museum stakeholder brief, authentic collection records, an empty advanced-selector region, interactive filters, nested catalog lists, and acceptance tests without equipment or transit source.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner builds and defends a distinct selector system through fifteen independent outcomes while retaining all prior CSS source, semantic heading, keyboard, and persistence invariants.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies only failed museum task, invariant, and observation; learner selects diagnosis and repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request lab hints point to competency and evidence category, then relevant artifact region after another attempt, never final selector.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed requirement creates a smaller changed collection case and parallel acceptance check before learner retries original artifact.

interactions.title: Build museum prerequisite baseline
interactions.learnerAction: Build valid museum rules, proportional basic hooks, and content-derived collection heading hierarchy before advanced matching.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent museum prerequisite baseline is saved.

evidence.observable: Rules parse, basic sets are exact, IDs remain unique, and unstyled headings navigate coherent collection structure.
evidence.changedCase: A new wing and collection category enter hierarchy.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Advanced selectors can substitute for correct source and heading architecture.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair grammar, hooks, and hierarchy before composition.

interactions.title: Build museum relationship selectors
interactions.learnerAction: Build selector lists and exact descendant, child, adjacent, or subsequent sibling relations from museum content requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent relationship rule set is saved.

evidence.observable: Every selector branch matches complete intended set and excludes unrelated nested or sibling records.
evidence.changedCase: Wrappers, nested records, and intervening notes are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Workshop selectors can be renamed for museum transfer.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive each relation from museum tree and task.

interactions.title: Test museum tree mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Insert, wrap, reorder, and remove museum records while proving each list, compound, logical, and combinator selector's exact subjects.

evidence.observable: Match sets change only when required subject or tree relationship changes.
evidence.changedCase: One object moves deeper, another changes parent, and sibling adjacency breaks.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Current shallow tree proves future relationship behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Re-evaluate final subject and every tree edge after mutation.

interactions.title: Build museum attribute contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Choose and author attribute operators and case policies for museum availability, media, language, collection tokens, and destinations.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent museum attribute rules are saved.

evidence.observable: Operators match complete semantic value grammar and reject empty, partial, case, path, query, fragment, and token near misses.
evidence.changedCase: Vocabulary and destination values expand.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Substring matching is universal independent solution.
interactions.correctionRoute: Write accepted and rejected value grammar before selector.

interactions.title: Test museum attribute mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate every museum attribute contract through DOM and prove exact matched and computed changes without reload.

evidence.observable: Each mutation affects only selectors whose current operator condition changes.
evidence.changedCase: Presence, exact value, tokens, language, and URL all mutate live.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Serialized source is only match input.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect current DOM attribute and operator truth.

interactions.title: Build museum interaction states
interactions.learnerAction: Build visible focus, focus-within, hover, active, link, target, and disclosure state styling for museum navigation and filters.

evidence.observable: Styles follow actual keyboard, pointer, URL, and native disclosure states without hiding information.
evidence.changedCase: Input method, target fragment, focus path, and disclosure state change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hover and permanent classes are enough for interaction state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use real state owner and test every supported input path.

interactions.title: Test museum form states
interactions.learnerAction: Test museum search and filter controls across enabled, disabled, required, optional, valid, invalid, user-invalid, range, checked, and reset states.

evidence.observable: Current native state controls matches while labels and errors remain persistent HTML.
evidence.changedCase: Controls traverse untouched, changed, invalid, corrected, disabled, checked, and reset states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: State styling itself supplies form meaning and error recovery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate native state, presentation, and associated information.

interactions.title: Test museum structural states
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate museum collection children and verify empty, first, last, only, nth-child, nth-of-type, and relational state selectors.

evidence.observable: Matches follow actual child list, type filtering, and relative anchored content.
evidence.changedCase: Records insert, reorder, change type, wrap, and become empty.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Structural pseudo-classes refer to visual row positions.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate DOM element children and selector filters.

interactions.title: Build museum presentational fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Build bounded museum ::before, ::after, ::selection, ::placeholder, ::first-line, or ::marker treatments with persistent task information.

evidence.observable: Each fragment has valid origin and presentational purpose while headings, names, labels, instructions, and states remain authored.
evidence.changedCase: Text, wrapping, selection, entered value, and marker state change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generated fragments can supply missing museum content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist meaning first and use fragment only for presentation.

interactions.title: Test museum fragment absence
interactions.learnerAction: Suppress generated content and user-agent styling variations, then verify museum heading navigation, names, instructions, states, copy, and tasks.

evidence.observable: Required content and relationships remain available in named test environments without generated presentation.
evidence.changedCase: Generated content support and user style change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Current visual support proves pseudo-elements are semantic.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect authored source and bound claims to named environments.

interactions.title: Build museum semantic lists
interactions.learnerAction: Build ordered handling procedures, unordered collections, and nested catalog groups whose meaning remains correct before marker styling.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent museum list source is saved.

evidence.observable: List type follows order meaning and item ownership remains valid through nesting.
evidence.changedCase: A collection group becomes a required handling sequence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS marker type determines whether list is ordered.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose HTML semantics from order consequences.

interactions.title: Build museum marker and counter rules
interactions.learnerAction: Style museum markers and optional section counters without hard-coded sequence text or replacing headings and list items.
interactions.artifactChange: Museum marker and counter presentation is saved.

evidence.observable: Markers originate from list items and counters track scoped source order while persistent semantics remain authored.
evidence.changedCase: New wing, section, list item, and nested group are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Counters are semantic replacements for headings and ordered lists.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep semantic source, then derive optional representation.

interactions.title: Test museum list mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Test museum insertion, removal, reorder, reversed, start, item value, nesting, long marker, unsupported representation, copy, and style-disabled cases.

evidence.observable: Semantic order and understandable representation remain correct across every changed case.
evidence.changedCase: Procedure reverses, begins at twelve, gains nested items, and exceeds marker symbol range.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One initial numbering screenshot proves list resilience.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect semantic tree, counter state, marker output, and alternate rendering.

interactions.title: Run integrated museum mutation
interactions.learnerAction: Change museum hierarchy, wrapper depth, sibling order, attribute values, focus, validity, target, generated content, and list sequence in one regression.

evidence.observable: Every rule changes only for its named condition while semantic and task invariants remain stable.
evidence.changedCase: All matching and semantic dimensions mutate from one passing baseline.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Passing isolated selectors guarantees combined artifact.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace first divergence by dependency and rerun downstream evidence.

interactions.title: Defend museum selector system
interactions.learnerAction: Defend independent museum grammar, hooks, hierarchy, relations, operators, states, fragments, list semantics, counters, mutations, tradeoffs, limits, and next tests.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent museum evidence package is submitted.

evidence.observable: Every decision cites direct current and changed evidence, rejected broader alternatives, named environment, and bounded conclusion.
evidence.changedCase: Stakeholder adds new wing, record state, filter, list sequence, and presentation constraint.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Many selectors and polished museum visuals prove mastery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Connect each source decision to exact match and retained user-task evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed retrieval record reconstructs selector grammar, subjects, relationships, attribute contracts, state sources, fragment limits, and list sequences from mixed unfamiliar evidence and schedules parallel correction.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner sees unfamiliar source, DOM trees, attributes, state timelines, render fragments, heading outlines, and list mutations without prior activity answers.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner retrieves all eight concepts, corrects misconceptions, and completes different parallel cases before review evidence is accepted.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies misconception and missing evidence category after commitment without revealing canonical answer before correction.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request review hints cue retrieval category, contrast two possible conditions, then provide a smaller parallel case.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every miss requires explanation, worked contrast, and different changed-case response before delayed review is rescheduled.

interactions.title: Retrieve composed rule grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct selector-list, complex, compound, simple-selector, combinator, rule, block, declaration, property, and value boundaries.

evidence.observable: All syntax units are ordered and labeled without comma, whitespace, or block confusion.
evidence.changedCase: Mixed source gains another selector branch and declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Commas separate declarations and spaces separate list branches.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace selector prelude and declaration block independently.

interactions.title: Retrieve subjects and hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve exact type, class, and identifier subjects plus heading ranks for an unfamiliar semantic tree before CSS fragments appear.
interactions.artifactChange: Basic subject and heading answer is saved.

evidence.observable: Literal subjects and content-derived heading outline remain correct with author styles disabled.
evidence.changedCase: A subsection moves and class membership changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generated heading text and selector name establish semantic hierarchy.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect authored heading source and literal selector tokens.

interactions.title: Predict relationship selector sets
interactions.learnerAction: Predict selector-list, descendant, child, adjacent-sibling, subsequent-sibling, and bounded :has() subjects on a changed tree.

evidence.observable: Each final subject satisfies exact list branch and tree relation after wrapper and sibling mutations.
evidence.changedCase: Wrapper, intervening sibling, and relative child are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All relationship selectors mean related somewhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Walk exact tree edges from final subject.

interactions.title: Correct list-versus-descendant confusion
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a claim that comma and whitespace match the same elements and repair one invalid regular selector list.

evidence.observable: Correction distinguishes OR union, ancestor relation, and whole-list invalidation.
evidence.changedCase: One branch becomes invalid and one subject moves outside ancestor.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invalid list branch is always ignored and whitespace means OR.
interactions.correctionRoute: Parse list production, then evaluate each valid branch.

interactions.title: Retrieve attribute operator contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve presence, exact, token, language-prefix, prefix, suffix, substring, default case, i, and s matching contracts.
interactions.artifactChange: Attribute operator retrieval table is saved.

evidence.observable: Every operator has correct accepted and rejected value shapes including empty and mixed-case cases.
evidence.changedCase: Values gain tokens, paths, queries, fragments, and uppercase text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All operators perform loose substring search.
interactions.correctionRoute: State exact value grammar and comparison rule.

interactions.title: Correct attribute semantic overclaim
interactions.learnerAction: Correct the claim that an attribute selector creates state and that broad matching plus important declaration guarantees intended behavior.

evidence.observable: Correction separates attribute string match, actual semantic state, and deferred cascade precedence.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute value changes without control state and control state changes without value string.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selector syntax creates semantics and precedence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect DOM attribute, browser state, and computed result separately.

interactions.title: Retrieve pseudo-class state sources
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve owners and transitions for focus, hover, active, link, target, open, form, language, empty, and child-index pseudo-classes.

evidence.observable: Each match begins and ends from actual browser, URL, document, control, language, or tree change.
evidence.changedCase: Input, fragment, disclosure, validity, language, and structure mutate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-classes create their named states.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name state model and trigger before selector.

interactions.title: Predict focus form and structure
interactions.learnerAction: Predict focus-visible, focus-within, user-invalid, checked, nth-child, and nth-of-type matches through a changed interaction timeline.
interactions.artifactChange: Mixed state timeline prediction is saved.

evidence.observable: Matches follow current input modality, ancestor focus, user validity interaction, checkedness, and sibling indices.
evidence.changedCase: Focus method, value, checkedness, and sibling types change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All focus, validity, and nth selectors are static source attributes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Replay actual state and tree changes in order.

interactions.title: Retrieve pseudo-element boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve originating element and render conditions for before, after, marker, first-line, first-letter, selection, and placeholder fragments.

evidence.observable: Fragments remain distinct from DOM nodes and required heading, label, instruction, and state content.
evidence.changedCase: Text reflows, input fills, selection moves, and generated content disappears.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-elements are authored semantic children.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare DOM, render fragment, and persistent task content.

interactions.title: Correct generated-content substitution
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a design that supplies heading, form instruction, and required status only through generated content.

evidence.observable: Required content becomes authored and related while pseudo-elements remain nonessential presentation.
evidence.changedCase: Generated content is unavailable and required wording changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS alternative text universally replaces semantic HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Persist task content and bound fragment use.

interactions.title: Retrieve list and counter evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve ordered versus unordered meaning, item tree order, reversed, start, value, nesting, marker box, counter scope, and changed sequence evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: List and counter evidence is reconstructed.

evidence.observable: Semantic list and current marker or counter sequence remain distinct and understandable.
evidence.changedCase: List reverses, starts differently, inserts items, nests, and exceeds marker range.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible marker alone determines list meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect HTML list semantics before representation.

interactions.title: Explain integrated selector correction
interactions.learnerAction: Explain and correct mixed invalid rule, wrong subject, hierarchy loss, relation overreach, value collision, impossible state, generated instruction, and numbering drift.
interactions.artifactChange: Integrated correction and delayed schedule are saved.

evidence.observable: Correction follows dependency order, preserves semantics, retests changed cases, and completes a parallel trace.
evidence.changedCase: Defects recombine in a different order with similar output.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One rewritten selector system proves every cause resolved.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair first divergence and rerun downstream match evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A no-hint performance bank samples independent grammar, hierarchy, match prediction, coding, live mutation, state diagnosis, fragment judgment, list sequencing, and defense across the complete module boundary.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives secure parallel source, trees, attributes, state timelines, render fragments, headings, and lists unrelated to prior scenarios.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner submits fifteen independently graded evidence responses; misses route after closure to correction and a distinct secure reassessment.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: During assessment, feedback records only submission and observable check result; diagnostic explanation appears after form closure without exposing reusable canonical answers.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No instructional hints, syntax completions, answer elimination, or canonical source are available during assessment.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failed concepts route after closure to targeted worked contrast, changed practice, and secure parallel reassessment with distinct source and evidence.

interactions.title: Label composed rule grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Label selector list, complex and compound selectors, combinators, simple selectors, rule, block, declarations, properties, and values in secure source.

evidence.observable: Every syntax unit receives correct parsing and matching role.
evidence.changedCase: Secure source adds another branch and declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Whitespace and comma have same grammar role.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction separates prelude and block.

interactions.title: Repair subjects and hierarchy
interactions.learnerAction: Repair secure type, class, identifier, and heading source without replacing semantic elements or creating duplicate identity.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure source and hierarchy repair is submitted.

evidence.observable: Basic subject sets are proportional and unstyled heading navigation remains coherent.
evidence.changedCase: A subsection and repeated component are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: IDs and generated headings are safest precision tools.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction derives semantics and reuse first.

interactions.title: Predict secure relationship sets
interactions.learnerAction: Predict selector-list, descendant, child, adjacent, subsequent, and logical pseudo-class subjects on changed secure tree.

evidence.observable: Every final subject satisfies exact list branch and tree relation.
evidence.changedCase: Wrapper, intervening sibling, and relative child mutate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All relationship selectors match nearby content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction walks tree edges.

interactions.title: Debug secure relationship overreach
interactions.learnerAction: Repair invalid selector-list branch and overbroad descendant or sibling relation without adding redundant hooks.

evidence.observable: Rule parses and matches only subjects in required relationship after changed nesting.
evidence.changedCase: Another nested and intervening element enters fixture.

interactions.feedbackTarget: More classes or universal selector are valid relation repairs.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction encodes exact tree edge.

interactions.title: Predict secure attribute sets
interactions.learnerAction: Predict presence, exact, token, language-prefix, prefix, suffix, substring, and case-flag matches across secure near values.

evidence.observable: Every accepted and rejected value follows exact operator and case contract.
evidence.changedCase: Values gain empty, mixed-case, token, query, and fragment variants.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Substring is equivalent to all other operators.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction writes value grammar.

interactions.title: Implement secure attribute rules
interactions.learnerAction: Implement operators and case policies for unfamiliar closed value, token vocabulary, language, and destination contracts.
