evidence.observable: Rules match intended values and reject partial, empty, case, path, query, and fragment near misses.
evidence.changedCase: Vocabulary and URL shapes expand.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad matching is future-proof by default.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction tests complete near-miss set.

interactions.title: Identify secure state owners
interactions.learnerAction: Identify browser, URL, document, form, language, user-action, and tree owners for unfamiliar pseudo-class transitions.

evidence.observable: Each pseudo-class maps to actual trigger, duration, and termination.
evidence.changedCase: Input, fragment, disclosure, form, language, and structure mutate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pseudo-class creates its state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction models external state.

interactions.title: Implement accessible state styling
interactions.learnerAction: Implement focus, focus-within, pointer, target, disclosure, and form-state styling without hover-only information or hidden focus.

evidence.observable: Real states match across keyboard, pointer, touch, URL, disclosure, and form paths with persistent information.
evidence.changedCase: Input method and state sequence change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hover is universal interaction evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction tests every supported input path.

interactions.title: Debug secure impossible states
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose impossible pseudo-class conjunction and wrong child-index assumption, then repair from actual state and tree evidence.

evidence.observable: Repaired selectors match observable compatible states and correct sibling indices.
evidence.changedCase: Control states and sibling types change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Valid syntax guarantees possible match.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction enumerates states and tree.

interactions.title: Classify pseudo-element uses
interactions.learnerAction: Classify unfamiliar before, after, marker, first-line, selection, and placeholder proposals as bounded presentation or required-content misuse.

evidence.observable: Required headings, names, instructions, states, and controls remain authored; fragments stay presentational.
evidence.changedCase: Generated content is suppressed and text reflows.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All generated text is semantic if announced once.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction tests fragment absence.

interactions.title: Implement bounded fragments
interactions.learnerAction: Implement unfamiliar decorative and text-fragment styling while preserving persistent headings, labels, instructions, state text, and tasks.

evidence.observable: Pseudo-elements originate correctly, use supported properties, and can disappear without information loss.
evidence.changedCase: Text, selection, input value, and generated-content availability change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Required content belongs in content property.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction persists meaning in HTML.

interactions.title: Choose semantic list models
interactions.learnerAction: Choose ordered or unordered list semantics and marker or counter strategy for unfamiliar sequences and categories.

evidence.observable: List type follows order meaning and presentation strategy preserves semantic source.
evidence.changedCase: Category becomes required sequence and gains nesting.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Marker shape determines semantic list type.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction tests reorder meaning.

interactions.title: Implement marker and counter system
interactions.learnerAction: Implement secure semantic lists, marker styling, and scoped counters without hard-coded numbering or heading replacement.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure marker and counter implementation is submitted.

evidence.observable: Current list and counter sequence follows source, nesting, reset, increment, and representation.
evidence.changedCase: Items and sections insert at multiple depths.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Hard-coded generated numbers are maintainable sequence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction derives numbers from semantic state.

interactions.title: Debug secure sequence drift
interactions.learnerAction: Repair list numbering, nesting, marker-range, generated-content, and style-disabled failures after unfamiliar insertions and reversal.

evidence.observable: Semantic and presented sequence remains understandable through insertion, reversal, start, nesting, and representation limits.
evidence.changedCase: List reverses, starts at nine, inserts items, and exceeds chosen symbols.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Manual renumbering is sufficient repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction restores semantic or scoped counter source.

interactions.title: Defend complete selector behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Defend unfamiliar rule grammar, subjects, heading hierarchy, relations, attribute contracts, state transitions, fragment boundaries, list semantics, counters, and changed cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure integrated selector defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Every claim cites exact subject, condition, source, state owner, mutation, semantic invariant, environment, and evidence limit.
evidence.changedCase: Tree, values, focus, validity, generated content, hierarchy, and sequences all change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selector source complexity and visual result prove independent mastery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction rebuilds evidence chain and parallel reassessment.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent CSS subject, browser-interoperability, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, assistive-technology, responsive, privacy, and duplication reviews remain incomplete.

Learner-facing explanations, semantic source fixtures, DOM mutation fixtures, canonical answers, executable match and state checks, browser and assistive-technology matrices, persisted correction schedules, and learner pilots remain unauthored.
